pattern: Greedy after sorting by right endpoint then descending left: process intervals and, whenever one lacks two chosen points, add the largest still-available points (its top two) so they help the most future intervals.
hints: Sort the intervals so you finish the ones that end earliest first, breaking ties by starting later.
Keep the two largest points you have already committed; for each interval count how many of them fall inside it.
If an interval already contains two chosen points do nothing; if it contains one, add its right end; if none, add its two rightmost values, since high points are reusable by later intervals.

edge_inputs.description: chained intervals forcing distinct point pairs
single interval needs exactly two points
minimal-width interval pins two adjacent values
nested intervals all satisfied by the innermost pair
disjoint intervals each need their own pair

stress.description: random intervals over a modest coordinate range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    intervals = []
    for _ in range(n):
        a = rng.randint(0, 200)
        b = rng.randint(a + 1, a + 30)
        intervals.append([a, b])
    return [intervals]
stress.note: ensures start < end and overlapping ranges to exercise reuse
